steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. Bennett tells stories about crossing the English Channel in rough weather — but the details keep shifting: sometimes it was him and the captain, sometimes him and the second mate, sometimes him alone. One constant remains: in every version, Bennett was among those who were not ill. The explanation must capture both the shifting details and the consistent self-flattery.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — During a previous boat trip, Bennett spent more time with the other passengers than with the captain.: this misreads the passage; Bennett's relationship with the captain is not the point. ✗
  • Choice B — Bennett has a hard time remembering his first trip across the English Channel when his friends ask about it.: the passage shows intentional revision, not genuine memory failure. ✗
  • Choice C — When Bennett speaks of an earlier trip, he often changes the details but always brags about his own wellness.: captures both the shifting details and the one constant — Bennett always comes out as the well one. ✓
  • Choice D — Bennett is worried that he and his friends will encounter rough waters during their boat trip.: Bennett's worry about the current trip is not mentioned in the passage. ✗
That matches Choice C — When Bennett speaks of an earlier trip, he often changes the details but always brags about his own wellness..
